evidence-binding: n2-codex-inheritance-facts b2d4e33f1541
findings: |
  Outcome
  - Yes. For a named custom agent/role file, omitting both top-level `model` and `model_reasoning_effort` is the documented inherit mechanism. The current official Subagents manual says those optional fields "inherit from the parent session when you omit them." Installed Codex CLI 0.144.3 also passed a scratch-only runtime probe: parent and identity-selected child each persisted `payload.model = "gpt-5.6-sol"` and `payload.effort = "high"` in their own `turn_context` JSONL record.
  - No documented explicit inherit value/sentinel was found for either role-file key. Current docs consistently say to omit the field; the spawn tool schema likewise says to omit `reasoning_effort` to inherit. The 0.144.3 role parser flattens ordinary `ConfigToml` keys and the spawn code represents the choices as optional values; no special `inherit` branch exists. Therefore do not write `model = "inherit"`, `model_reasoning_effort = "inherit"`, `null`, or an empty string and call that inheritance.

  Configuration and spawn ordering
  - A named CLI configuration profile (`--profile NAME`) is a startup layer: base user config is loaded, then `$CODEX_HOME/NAME.config.toml`; overall documented precedence is CLI/`-c` > trusted project `.codex/config.toml` > named profile > user config > system config > built-ins. Since 0.134.0, legacy `[profiles.NAME]` and top-level `profile = NAME` are not supported.
  - Custom agent definitions are discovered while session configuration is loaded, from the `agents/` directory belonging to each config layer (including `$CODEX_HOME/agents/` and trusted project `.codex/agents/`). A role is selected by its `name`, not necessarily its filename.
  - At a non-full-history spawn in 0.144.3, the handler: (1) snapshots the parent's effective live turn, including model and effort; (2) applies requested spawn `model`/`reasoning_effort` overrides; (3) applies the selected named role as a high-precedence session-flags config layer; then (4) reapplies runtime-only approval/cwd/permission values. Consequently an explicit model/effort in the role is locked and wins over a spawn request; omission is what leaves inherited parent values available. A full-history fork is a different path: it rejects `agent_type`, `model`, or `reasoning_effort` overrides and inherits all three. For an identity-selected role gate use `fork_turns = "none"` (or a non-full-history partial fork), not the V2 default `all`.
  - Negative boundary: source ordering establishes the above for 0.144.3, but config text alone is not proof that the intended identity was used. In the first bounded prompt, the model used `task_name = "inherit_probe"` but omitted `agent_type`; the child `session_meta` consequently had `agent_role: null`. The corrected prompt required the `agent_type` field, and the durable child metadata then recorded `agent_role: "inherit_probe"`. Any gate must inspect metadata and fail closed if the identity is absent.

  Exact durable JSONL locations
  - Parent and child are separate files under `$CODEX_HOME/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-<UTC timestamp>-<thread-id>.jsonl` (the installed run used `/tmp/codex-inherit-gate-home/sessions/2026/07/15/...`).
  - In each file, inspect the line with top-level `type == "turn_context"`: the effective model is `.payload.model`; the effective reasoning effort is `.payload.effort`. The Rust serialization is `RolloutLine { timestamp, #[flatten] RolloutItem }`, so these are not nested under `.payload.turn_context`. `effort` may be absent when its optional value is `None`; `model` is required.
  - Identify the child from its first `session_meta` line: `.payload.parent_thread_id` equals the parent's thread id, `.payload.thread_source == "subagent"`, `.payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.agent_role` equals the requested role identity, and `.payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.agent_path`/`.payload.agent_path` give its canonical path. The parent `response_item` for `spawn_agent` is useful corroboration (`.payload.name == "spawn_agent"` and its arguments contain `agent_type`), but child `session_meta` is the decisive identity evidence.
  - `codex exec --json` stdout is an event stream, not the complete durable session evidence; in the probe it did not print the child `turn_context`. Read the per-thread rollout JSONL files instead.

  Reproducible main-session gate (writes only out-of-repo scratch)
  1. Record the bound: `codex --version` (expected here: `codex-cli 0.144.3`). Create scratch: `GATE_HOME=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/codex-inherit-gate.XXXXXX")`; `GATE_WORK=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/codex-inherit-work.XXXXXX")`; `mkdir -p "$GATE_HOME/agents"`. Make authentication available without copying/printing secrets, for example `ln -s "$HOME/.codex/auth.json" "$GATE_HOME/auth.json"`; optionally symlink `models_cache.json` too.
  2. Put this in `$GATE_HOME/config.toml`: `model = "gpt-5.6-sol"`, `model_reasoning_effort = "high"`, then `[agents]`, `max_threads = 2`, `max_depth = 1`. The chosen model/effort can be replaced with any supported parent pair whose exact values you want to test.
  3. Put this in `$GATE_HOME/agents/inherit_probe.toml`, deliberately with neither key: `name = "inherit_probe"`; `description = "Scratch-only inheritance probe."`; `developer_instructions = "Return exactly: probe-complete"`.
  4. Launch from scratch, never the worktree: `CODEX_HOME="$GATE_HOME" codex -s read-only -a never -C "$GATE_WORK" exec --skip-git-repo-check --json 'Call collaboration.spawn_agent exactly once with agent_type set to inherit_probe, task_name set to role_identity_probe, fork_turns set to none, and no model or reasoning_effort. Wait for it.'`
  5. Emit identity plus values from every rollout: `find "$GATE_HOME/sessions" -type f -name '*.jsonl' -print | while IFS= read -r file; do jq -c 'select(.type=="session_meta" or .type=="turn_context") | if .type=="session_meta" then {type,id:.payload.id,parent:.payload.parent_thread_id,thread_source:.payload.thread_source,role:(try .payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.agent_role catch null),path:.payload.agent_path} else {type,model:.payload.model,effort:.payload.effort} end' "$file"; done`.
  6. PASS only when exactly one root file has no parent, exactly one child file points to that root, child role is exactly `inherit_probe`, and the root and child `turn_context` model/effort pairs are byte-equal to each other and to the intended parent pair. FAIL on missing/extra children, `agent_role: null`, a full-history override rejection, absent `turn_context`, or any model/effort mismatch. Preserve the scratch directory long enough for audit, then remove it; the worktree remains untouched.

  Runtime observation (bounded)
  - Platform/runtime: installed npm `@openai/codex` 0.144.3, macOS arm64 binary, MultiAgentV2, 2026-07-15. Exact tag commit: `78ad6e6bfd1d3b6a209acd3ef82172a96b25179c` (`rust-v0.144.3`).
  - Corrected run parent thread `019f637f-a0b7-7ef3-997d-c034b59d6b00` persisted `{model:"gpt-5.6-sol", effort:"high"}`. Child thread `019f637f-c6a3-7911-b6c1-6040da274d31` persisted `agent_role:"inherit_probe"`, parent linkage to that root, and the same pair. The role file omitted both keys. This verifies the documented omission behavior for this version/environment without writing the issue worktree.

  Version bounds and uncertainty
  - Documentation was freshly retrieved on 2026-07-15 and describes current Codex releases. Source and experiment claims are bounded to CLI 0.144.3/tag `rust-v0.144.3`; the profile-format migration is explicitly documented for 0.134.0+. Do not extrapolate the precise spawn ordering or JSONL schema to older releases without rerunning the gate.
  - The experiment verifies one supported model/effort pair and one standalone role identity. It does not prove every model supports every effort, or that hosted ChatGPT Work persists the same local JSONL layout.
sources: |
  - OpenAI Codex Manual, "Subagents" / custom agents (current manual; retrieved 2026-07-15): https://learn.chatgpt.com/docs/agent-configuration/subagents.md
  - OpenAI Codex Manual, "Advanced Configuration" / profiles (current manual; retrieved 2026-07-15): https://learn.chatgpt.com/docs/config-file/config-advanced.md
  - OpenAI Codex Manual, "Config basics" / configuration precedence (current manual; retrieved 2026-07-15): https://learn.chatgpt.com/docs/config-file/config-basic.md
  - OpenAI Codex source, 0.144.3 role application and high-precedence role layer (retrieved 2026-07-15): https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.3/codex-rs/core/src/agent/role.rs
  - OpenAI Codex source, 0.144.3 V2 spawn ordering (retrieved 2026-07-15): https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.3/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents_v2/spawn.rs
  - OpenAI Codex source, 0.144.3 parent snapshot, explicit override, full-history rejection (retrieved 2026-07-15): https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.3/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents_common.rs
  - OpenAI Codex source, 0.144.3 standalone role discovery/parser (retrieved 2026-07-15): https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.3/codex-rs/core/src/config/agent_roles.rs
  - OpenAI Codex source, 0.144.3 `RolloutItem`, `RolloutLine`, and `TurnContextItem` JSON schema (retrieved 2026-07-15): https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.3/codex-rs/protocol/src/protocol.rs
  - OpenAI Codex source, 0.144.3 mapping of live turn model/effort into persisted `TurnContextItem` (retrieved 2026-07-15): https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.3/codex-rs/core/src/session/turn_context.rs
  - Local installed CLI/help and scratch runtime probe, `codex-cli 0.144.3`, observed 2026-07-15; artifacts intentionally located under `/tmp/codex-inherit-gate-home`, outside the repository worktree.
delegation_outcome: completed
